  "signature_insights": [
    {
      "insight": "SGR (Schema-Guided Reasoning) forces models to choose a tool via structured output, eliminating the 'fork' where small models fail to call tools, making it a distinct agent architecture rather than just a prompt pattern.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Provides a robust architectural pattern for building reliable agents, especially when using smaller or local models.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "design_agentic_dev_workflow"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "distinctive",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0118",
        "P0118.C0005"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "insight": "Claude Skills (markdown instructions loaded on demand) save massive amounts of context tokens compared to MCPs, which load all tool schemas upfront and bloat the context window.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Crucial for optimizing token costs and context window efficiency in agentic workflows.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "optimize_inference_cost_latency",
        "design_agentic_dev_workflow"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "distinctive",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0119",
        "P0231"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "insight": "LLMs fail at bulk sorting due to positional bias. Using TrueSkill batch comparisons yields much higher correlation with real-world quality/virality.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Provides a mathematically sound method for using LLMs to evaluate and sort large datasets.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "build_ai_dev_eval"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "useful_packaging",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0156"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.9
    },
    {
      "insight": "Models of the same family (e.g., Claude reviewing Claude) exhibit 'Prior Prejudice' and will approve flawed code because they recognize their own generation patterns. Cross-model review is essential.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Fundamental principle for designing reliable AI code review pipelines.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "design_agentic_dev_workflow",
        "build_ai_dev_eval"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "distinctive",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0174",
        "P0240"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "insight": "Large universal AGENTS.md files are an anti-pattern that wastes tokens. Context should be modular, using skills and nested rules to load only what is necessary.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Best practice for managing context in AI coding assistants like Cursor and Claude Code.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "design_agentic_dev_workflow",
        "optimize_inference_cost_latency"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "useful_packaging",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0203"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.9
    }
  ],

      "when_to_use": "When using Claude Code for implementation, to catch security and logic flaws that Claude might miss due to self-preference bias.",
